title: Name age cosmological status and weapon are unresolved
claim_or_observation: Current research does not establish one final name form age biological category community equivalence or historical sword type for Kanchanan
notes: Binding casting costume VFX weapon dialogue and publicity safeguard

title: Kanchanan and Kayasandihai travel together
claim_or_observation: Kayasandihai says she came with her husband Kanchanan from Kanchanapuram in the Vidyadhara world to the Potiya mountain region
notes: Verify place community and kinship vocabulary

title: Kanchanan supports Kayasandihai during the curse
claim_or_observation: He tries to take her through the sky brings fruits roots and vegetables and identifies a land route toward people who feed those in distress
notes: Preserve practical care and limits of his ability

title: Kanchanan returns during Indra festival
claim_or_observation: Kayasandihai says her husband comes to see her on the day of Indra festival and leaves counting the time until the curse ends
notes: Verify frequency duration and exact counting language

title: Kayasandihai is cured and leaves independently
claim_or_observation: After Manimekalai cures her hunger Kayasandihai directs her to the Ulaga Aravi and departs for the sky world
notes: Retain her agency separate from husband narrative

title: Kayasandihai dies beyond Kanchanan's knowledge
claim_or_observation: The Kanthil Pavai says Kayasandihai was swallowed by the guardian of the Vindhya mountain while returning through a forbidden route
notes: Verify route prohibition goddess name and death verb

title: Kanchanan approaches Manimekalai as his wife
claim_or_observation: Seeing Manimekalai in Kayasandihai's form he approaches lovingly recalls their years together and praises her
notes: Verify physical approach speech and recognition details

title: Unexpected response becomes evidence in his mind
claim_or_observation: The apparent wife does not respond to his praise and instead speaks to Udayakumaran leading him to interpret speech smiles and gaze as signs of a lover
notes: Observed behaviour and inference must remain distinct

title: Kanchanan conceals himself with a sword
claim_or_observation: His anger burns and he enters the temple with a shining sword and hides like a snake in a hole
notes: Creates duration opportunity and weapon continuity before killing

title: Kanchanan kills Udayakumaran at night
claim_or_observation: When the prince returns at midnight Kanchanan believes he has come for his wife rises behind him and strikes with the sword
notes: Exact wound and weapon action require Tamil review

title: The pillar figure corrects the mistaken identity
claim_or_observation: The Kanthil Pavai stops Kanchanan from approaching and tells him the woman is Manimekalai disguised as Kayasandihai

title: Fate and cruel action are stated together
claim_or_observation: The divine figure says Udayakumaran's fate brought his death but also tells Kanchanan he committed a cruel deed and will face its result

title: Kanchanan departs sorrowing
claim_or_observation: After hearing the revelation Kanchanan feels sorry for his deed and returns to his place in the sky
notes: Text does not establish completed repair or redemption

title: Long care hardens into possessive expectation
claim_or_observation: Repeated support and separation create understandable hope but become dangerous when Kanchanan treats resemblance and expected response as proof of marital entitlement

title: No fantasy racial coding for Vidyadhara identity
claim_or_observation: Sky-world difference cannot be represented automatically through wings metallic skin racialised makeup or generic northern celestial costume
notes: Binding casting VFX costume and publicity rule

title: Mistake jealousy and fate do not excuse lethal violence
claim_or_observation: Incomplete knowledge emotional pain and karmic convergence can explain the event without converting the strike into accident self-defence honour or justified protection
notes: Binding screenplay choreography edit music and publicity rule
